title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs attributes, filters, merge drivers, and content policy from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the path-attribute and content-transformation policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for path-attribute and content-transformation policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G23 is a review in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref. Competency probe: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.

Inspect the Gita Large Content Policy trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose ordinary git, lfs-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.

content.text: For Gita Large Content Policy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Competency probe: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Any large file automatically belongs in a pointer-based extension regardless of access and history needs.

This does not satisfy Gita Large Content Policy for path-attribute and content-transformation policy.
The record names the assumption, applies Gita Large Content Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Gita Large Content Policy record says path-attribute and content-transformation policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

steps.title: Record what required effort
